name: Behalf.bot — reMarkable
description: reMarkable tablet integration. Reads annotations, sends briefings OTA, and indexes books in Postgres pgvector for semantic query. Cloud-sync mode only (no USB required). Opt-in — only valuable for installers who use a reMarkable Paper tablet.

enabled.description: Master switch. If false the plugin is dormant. Requires REMARKABLE_TOKEN in the installer's secret store.

cloud_mode.description: Use cloud-sync API (OTA, no USB). Set false only for legacy USB-tethered setups.

embedding_table.description: Postgres table that receives document chunk embeddings. Must exist with (document_id, chunk_idx, text, token_count, embedding vector(768)) columns.

ollama_model.description: Ollama embedding model. Must produce 768-dim vectors.

briefing_push.description: Allow pushing briefing documents to the tablet OTA. Requires cloud_mode: true.

sync_heartbeat.description: Enable a daily heartbeat that pulls new highlights + annotations from the tablet and writes them to the chassis DB.

activation._note: Activation gated on installer enabling per chassis.config.yaml modules.remarkable.enabled. Requires REMARKABLE_TOKEN in Vaultwarden (canonical item name: 'Behalf.bot - reMarkable Cloud API token'). For installers without reMarkable hardware, leave enabled: false.

_chassis_v1_notes.mcp_wiring: When enabled, the chassis .mcp.json should add: { 'command': 'uvx', 'args': ['remarkable-mcp'], 'env': { 'REMARKABLE_TOKEN': '${REMARKABLE_TOKEN}' } }. The token is a JSON blob: {'devicetoken': '<JWT>', 'usertoken': ''}. Register via: uvx remarkable-mcp --register <one-time-code> (code from my.remarkable.com/device/desktop/connect).
_chassis_v1_notes.token_registration: One-time registration per install. Produces a persistent device token. The JWT does not expire unless explicitly revoked.
_chassis_v1_notes.denylist: plugins/remarkable/scripts/remarkable_denylist.py enforces a default-allow model with hard-deny folders (e.g. private journals). Edit plugins/remarkable/config/remarkable_denylist_config.py at install time to add installer-specific denylists.
_chassis_v1_notes.book_ingestion: ingest_remarkable_book.py downloads via the tablet's direct HTTP API (10.11.99.1) - requires USB or local network. Cloud MCP is used for annotation + browse; book download still needs the local API.
_chassis_v1_notes.ported_from: <v1-reference-install>#554 (cloud-sync MCP switch). New scripts adapted from <v1-reference-install> scripts/ at commit aligned with that PR.
